questions.question: What was Sven Olsen’s job in the space station?
questions.answer: He was a construction worker assembling structures in space.

questions.question: Why was Sven suitable for space work?
questions.answer: Because he was thin and lightweight.

questions.question: What attracted the narrator’s attention at first?
questions.answer: A musical whistle near his ear.

questions.question: What surprised the narrator when he looked up?
questions.answer: A small yellow bird floating in the air.

questions.question: What was the name of the canary in the story?
questions.answer: Claribel.

questions.question: Why did Sven bring Claribel to the station?
questions.answer: To observe how a bird behaves in zero gravity.

questions.question: How did Claribel move in the space station?
questions.answer: She floated and performed loops in the air.

questions.question: Why did the crew keep Claribel a secret?
questions.answer: Because pets were not officially allowed in the station.

questions.question: What happened to Claribel one morning?
questions.answer: She suddenly collapsed.

questions.question: How did the crew try to help Claribel?
questions.answer: They gave her oxygen.

questions.question: What did the narrator remember about canaries?
questions.answer: They were used in coal mines to detect poisonous gases.

questions.question: Why are canaries sensitive to danger?
questions.answer: They react quickly to poisonous gases.

questions.question: What machine malfunctioned in the station?
questions.answer: The air purifier.

questions.question: What was the role of the air purifier?
questions.answer: To maintain clean and safe air in the station.

questions.question: Why did the alarm system fail to warn the crew?
questions.answer: Because the second alarm circuit was not connected.

questions.question: What natural event caused part of the purifier to freeze?
questions.answer: An eclipse.

questions.question: What made the narrator realize there was danger?
questions.answer: Claribel collapsing.

questions.question: Why was Claribel called a 'feathered friend'?
questions.answer: Because she helped save the crew from danger.

questions.question: What lesson does the story give about observation?
questions.answer: Careful observation can reveal hidden dangers.

questions.question: What does the story suggest about technology?
questions.answer: Technology can fail sometimes.

questions.question: What role did curiosity play in the story?
questions.answer: Curiosity led Sven to bring Claribel to the station.

questions.question: What does Claribel symbolize in the story?
questions.answer: Nature helping humans detect danger.

questions.question: Why did the crew initially not suspect the air problem?
questions.answer: Because they trusted the automatic alarm system.

questions.question: What important discovery did the crew make after the incident?
questions.answer: That the air purifier had malfunctioned.

questions.question: What is the central message of 'Feathered Friend'?
questions.answer: Observation and awareness can help detect danger and save lives.
